questions.question: Why can't we use block.timestamp for lottery randomness?
options: Block timestamps are always the same value
Miners control timestamps within a range and can choose to mine or skip blocks based on favorable outcomes
Block timestamps are too large for modulo operations
questions.correctAnswer: Miners control timestamps within a range and can choose to mine or skip blocks based on favorable outcomes

questions.question: In threshold randomness, no single operator can generate the random value alone - they need threshold cooperation.
What are the three guarantees a fair lottery needs?
When using future block hashes for randomness, the miner of that block can profitably ______ their own block to change the outcome.
How does dcipher's threshold randomness work?
options: One operator generates randomness and others verify it
Each operator generates a partial signature using their key share, which are combined when threshold is met
The smart contract generates randomness and operators validate it
Randomness is pulled from an external oracle service
questions.correctAnswer: Each operator generates a partial signature using their key share, which are combined when threshold is met

questions.question: The threshold signature itself IS the random value used for winner selection.
What happens during the randomness callback?
options: The contract generates its own random number
The RandomnessSender verifies the threshold signature and calls onRandomnessReceived() with the verified random value
The organizer manually inputs the random value
questions.correctAnswer: The RandomnessSender verifies the threshold signature and calls onRandomnessReceived() with the verified random value
